title: Cross product
short: For 3-D vectors, a new vector perpendicular to both, with length |u||v|sin θ.
full:
The cross product u×v of two three-dimensional vectors produces a third vector that is perpendicular to both of them. Its direction follows the right-hand rule, and its length, |u||v|sin θ, equals the area of the parallelogram the two vectors span — so a cross product of zero means the vectors are parallel.

Unlike the dot product, the cross product is only defined in 3-D (in 2-D it reduces to the single scalar u₁v₂ − u₂v₁), and it is anti-commutative: v×u = −(u×v).
formulas: u×v = (u₂v₃ − u₃v₂, u₃v₁ − u₁v₃, u₁v₂ − u₂v₁)
